w:r.w:t: School of Computer Science and Mathematics
Use in solution your own class Hash Table with Linked List

appears only once and is the first unique character)

Given an array of numbers and a target sum, find
of numbers that add up to the sum.
Given an array of integers, find the element that appears the
. If multiple elements have the same highest frequency, return any of them.
Check if Two Strings are Isomorphic
if the characters in one string can be replaced to get the second string while preserving order.
Given an array, find the first element that appears
Check if a Word is Formed by Concatenating Other Words
Given a dictionary of words and a target word, check if the target word can be formed by concatenating two words from the dictionary.
Find Common Elements in Three Sorted Arrays
Given three sorted arrays, find the elements that appear in
Given two unsorted arrays, find the
Given an array of integers, check if there exists a
subarray with a sum of zero
Given an unsorted array, find the length of the
Check if Two Strings are Anagrams
Given two strings, check if they are
(contain the same characters with the same frequency).
Find Pairs with Given XOR Value
Given an array of numbers, count how many pairs
Find All Anagrams in a String
Given two strings s and p, find all the start indices of p's anagrams in s.
Return the answer in any order.
An anagram of a string is another string that contains the same characters with the same frequencies, but possibly in a different order.
You must solve this using hashing (e.g., character frequency maps) and a sliding window approach.
" at 1, "ab" at 2 — all are anagrams of "ab".
Clearly label each exercise in your submission.
Exercises are due at the beginning of the next class.
#define TABLE_SIZE 10 // Size of the hash table
Node(int k, int v) : key(k), value(v), next(nullptr) {}
LinkedList table[TABLE_SIZE]; // Array of linked lists for chaining
